contents.value:
```swift
func print(_ items: Any..., separator: String = " ", terminator: String = "\n")
```
Writes textual representations of the given items to standard output.

```swift
func debugPrint(_ items: Any..., separator: String = " ", terminator: String = "\n")
```
Writes debug textual representations of the given items to standard output.

```swift
func dump<T>(_ value: T, name: String? = nil, indent: Int = 0, maxDepth: Int = .max, maxItems: Int = .max) -> T
```
Dumps an object's contents using its mirror to standard output.

```swift
func min<T: Comparable>(_ x: T, _ y: T) -> T
```
Returns the lesser of two comparable values.

```swift
func max<T: Comparable>(_ x: T, _ y: T) -> T
```
Returns the greater of two comparable values.

```swift
func abs<T: Comparable & SignedNumeric>(_ x: T) -> T
```
Returns the absolute value of the given number.

```swift
func stride<T>(from start: T, to end: T, by stride: T.Stride) -> StrideTo<T>
```
Returns a sequence from start to (but not including) end, stepping by the specified amount.

```swift
func zip<Sequence1, Sequence2>(_ sequence1: Sequence1, _ sequence2: Sequence2) -> Zip2Sequence<Sequence1, Sequence2>
```
Creates a sequence of pairs built out of two underlying sequences.

```swift
func map<T>(_ transform: (Element) throws -> T) rethrows -> [T]
```
Returns an array containing the results of mapping the given closure over the sequence's elements.

```swift
func filter(_ isIncluded: (Element) throws -> Bool) rethrows -> [Element]
```
Returns an array containing only the elements that satisfy the given predicate.

```swift
func reduce<Result>(_ initialResult: Result, _ nextPartialResult: (Result, Element) throws -> Result) rethrows -> Result
```
Returns the result of combining the sequence's elements using the given closure.

```swift
func compactMap<ElementOfResult>(_ transform: (Element) throws -> ElementOfResult?) rethrows -> [ElementOfResult]
```
Returns an array containing the non-nil results of calling the given transformation.

```swift
func flatMap<SegmentOfResult: Sequence>(_ transform: (Element) throws -> SegmentOfResult) rethrows -> [SegmentOfResult.Element]
```
Returns an array containing the concatenated results of calling the given transformation.

```swift
func sorted() -> [Element]
```
Returns the elements of the sequence, sorted.

```swift
func forEach(_ body: (Element) throws -> Void) rethrows
```
Calls the given closure on each element in the sequence in the same order as a for-in loop.

```swift
func contains(_ element: Element) -> Bool
```
Returns a Boolean value indicating whether the sequence contains the given element.

```swift
var first: Element? { get }
```
The first element of the collection, or nil if the collection is empty.

```swift
var last: Element? { get }
```
The last element of the collection, or nil if the collection is empty.

```swift
var count: Int { get }
```
The number of elements in the collection.

```swift
var isEmpty: Bool { get }
```
A Boolean value indicating whether the collection is empty.

```swift
mutating func append(_ newElement: Element)
```
Adds a new element at the end of the array.

```swift
mutating func insert(_ newElement: Element, at i: Int)
```
Inserts a new element at the specified position.

```swift
mutating func remove(at index: Int) -> Element
```
Removes and returns the element at the specified position.

```swift
mutating func removeAll(keepingCapacity keepCapacity: Bool = false)
```
Removes all elements from the array.

```swift
func enumerated() -> EnumeratedSequence<Self>
```
Returns a sequence of pairs (n, x), where n represents a consecutive integer starting at zero.

```swift
func prefix(_ maxLength: Int) -> Slice<Self>
```
Returns a subsequence, up to the specified maximum length, containing the initial elements.

```swift
func suffix(_ maxLength: Int) -> Slice<Self>
```
Returns a subsequence, up to the given maximum length, containing the final elements.

```swift
func joined(separator: String = "") -> String
```
Returns a new string by concatenating the elements of the sequence, adding the given separator.

```swift
func split(separator: Character, maxSplits: Int = .max, omittingEmptySubsequences: Bool = true) -> [Substring]
```
Returns the longest possible subsequences of the collection, around elements equal to the given element.

```swift
func replacingOccurrences(of target: String, with replacement: String) -> String
```
Returns a new string in which all occurrences of a target string are replaced by another given string.

```swift
func hasPrefix(_ prefix: String) -> Bool
```
Returns a Boolean value indicating whether the string begins with the specified prefix.

```swift
func hasSuffix(_ suffix: String) -> Bool
```
Returns a Boolean value indicating whether the string ends with the specified suffix.

```swift
func uppercased() -> String
```
Returns an uppercased version of the string.

```swift
func lowercased() -> String
```
Returns a lowercased version of the string.

```swift
func trimmingCharacters(in set: CharacterSet) -> String
```
Returns a new string made by removing from both ends of the string characters contained in a given character set.

```swift
func precondition(_ condition: @autoclosure () -> Bool, _ message: @autoclosure () -> String = "")
```
Checks a necessary condition for making forward progress.

```swift
func assert(_ condition: @autoclosure () -> Bool, _ message: @autoclosure () -> String = "")
```
Performs a traditional C-style assert with an optional message.

```swift
func fatalError(_ message: @autoclosure () -> String = "") -> Never
```
Unconditionally prints a given message and stops execution.

```swift
func type<T>(of value: T) -> T.Type
```
Returns the dynamic type of a value.

```swift
func withAnimation<Result>(_ animation: Animation? = .default, _ body: () throws -> Result) rethrows -> Result
```
Returns the result of recomputing the view's body with the provided animation.

```swift
struct Int: FixedWidthInteger, SignedInteger
```
A signed integer value type.

```swift
struct Double: FloatingPoint
```
A double-precision, floating-point value type.

```swift
struct Float: FloatingPoint
```
A single-precision, floating-point value type.

```swift
struct String: StringProtocol, Hashable, Codable
```
A Unicode string value.

```swift
struct Bool: Sendable, Hashable, Codable
```
A value type whose instances are either true or false.

```swift
struct Array<Element>: RandomAccessCollection, MutableCollection
```
An ordered, random-access collection.

```swift
struct Dictionary<Key: Hashable, Value>: Collection
```
A collection whose elements are key-value pairs.

```swift
struct Set<Element: Hashable>: Collection
```
An unordered collection of unique elements.

```swift
enum Optional<Wrapped>: ExpressibleByNilLiteral
```
A type that represents either a wrapped value or the absence of a value.

```swift
enum Result<Success, Failure: Error>
```
A value that represents either a success or a failure, including an associated value in each case.

```swift
struct Character: Hashable, Sendable
```
A single extended grapheme cluster.

```swift
struct Data: Collection, ContiguousBytes, Hashable
```
A byte buffer in memory.

```swift
struct URL: Hashable, Equatable, Sendable
```
A value that identifies the location of a resource.

```swift
struct Date: Comparable, Hashable, Codable, Sendable
```
A specific point in time, independent of any calendar or time zone.

```swift
protocol Error
```
A type representing an error value that can be thrown.

```swift
typealias Codable = Decodable & Encodable
```
A type that can convert itself into and out of an external representation.

```swift
protocol Hashable: Equatable
```
A type that can be hashed into a Hasher to produce an integer hash value.

```swift
protocol Equatable
```
A type that can be compared for value equality.

```swift
protocol Comparable: Equatable
```
A type that can be compared using the relational operators.

```swift
protocol Sendable
```
A type whose values can safely be passed across concurrency domains by copying.

```swift
protocol AsyncSequence
```
A type that provides asynchronous, sequential, iterated access to its elements.

```swift
struct Task<Success: Sendable, Failure: Error>
```
A unit of asynchronous work.

```swift
typealias Void = ()
```
The return type of functions that don't explicitly specify a return type.

```swift
enum Never
```
The return type of functions that do not return normally; a type with no values.
The protocol to which all types implicitly conform.

```swift
protocol AnyObject
```
The protocol to which all classes implicitly conform.

```swift
struct AnyHashable: Hashable
```
A type-erased hashable value.

```swift
struct Range<Bound: Comparable>
```
A half-open interval from a lower bound up to, but not including, an upper bound.

```swift
struct ClosedRange<Bound: Comparable>
```
An interval from a lower bound up to, and including, an upper bound.

```swift
protocol Sequence
```
A type that provides sequential, iterated access to its elements.

```swift
protocol Collection: Sequence
```
A sequence whose elements can be traversed multiple times and accessed by indexed subscript.

```swift
class JSONDecoder
```
An object that decodes instances of a data type from JSON objects.

```swift
class JSONEncoder
```
An object that encodes instances of a data type as JSON objects.

```swift
class URLSession
```
An object that coordinates a group of related network data transfer tasks.

```swift
class DispatchQueue: DispatchObject
```
An object that manages the execution of work items serially or concurrently.

```swift
class NotificationCenter
```
A notification dispatch mechanism that enables the broadcast of information to registered observers.

```swift
class UserDefaults
```
An interface to the user's defaults database for persistent key-value storage.
